title: Gritty Mexican Pickle Quesadilla
description: A salty, crunchy quesadilla that fuses Veracruz flavors with Branston pickle and beurre-monte, perfect for a quick one-pot meal.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a nonstick skillet over medium heat.
Sprinkle the corn tortilla with salt and pepper, then place in pan until lightly browned, about 1 minute per side; remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the diced Branston pickle, smoked paprika, and cumin and cook for 2 minutes to bloom the spices.
Remove pan from heat and mix in shredded cheddar, monterey jack, parmesan, cilantro, and lime juice until combined.
Place a tortilla back in the pan, spoon half of the cheese mixture on one half, fold the tortilla over, and press gently.
Cook the folded quesadilla for 2-3 minutes per side until the cheese melts and the tortilla is golden and crisp.
Repeat with remaining tortilla and filling; serve hot with a drizzle of beurre‑monte and extra cilantro.
